first_half.read: Lakers led despite hot shooting profile; model treated LAL 55% first-half 3P as regression candidate, but failed to price the 107.5 hook and OKC-driven game-speed expansion correctly.

third_quarter.read: OKC flipped the game through pressure defense, live-ball creation, and efficient shot generation. Lakers turnovers produced a possession-quality collapse.

fourth_quarter.read: The game entered an OKC avalanche state. Thunder continued scoring aggressively even after separation, while Lakers still reached the team-total threshold by one basket despite losing control of the game.

OKC.Ajay_Mitchell: 24 points, 10 assists, 3 steals; major second-half pressure and creation engine.
OKC.Shai_Gilgeous_Alexander: 23 points, 9 assists, plus-24; controlled pace despite inefficient first half.
OKC.Chet_Holmgren: 18 points, 9 rebounds; efficient interior and rim pressure.
OKC.Isaiah_Hartenstein: 12 points, 9 rebounds, plus-26; second-half interior stabilizer.
OKC.Isaiah_Joe: 12 points on 4 made threes; garbage/extension scoring risk confirmed.

LAL.Rui_Hachimura: 21 points on 5 made threes; first-half shooting spike cooled but full-game total still barely cleared.
LAL.LeBron_James: 19 points, 8 assists, minus-24; late-game margin stress and turnover drag.
LAL.Austin_Reaves: 17 points, 9 assists, 5 turnovers; pressure vulnerability drove OKC runouts.
LAL.Luke_Kennard: 18 points; bench shooting kept LAL team-total alive despite game collapse.

model_diagnostic.failure_type: The model correctly saw LAL first-half shooting regression, but underestimated the probability that LAL could still reach 108 through residual scoring, free throws, bench minutes, and garbage-time extension.
why_the_bet_lost: LAL needed only 49 second-half points to beat 107.5 and landed exactly on 49.
The under needed 48 or fewer second-half points, leaving no cushion for normal playoff extension or late bench scoring.
OKC's second-half scoring avalanche created more possessions and forced Lakers response scoring despite game state worsening.
The model overweighted Lakers 3P regression and underweighted OKC pace/pressure spillover into opponent scoring volume.
The entry failed the newly-required playoff team-total under buffer standard.

patches_to_activate.rule: Do not fire playoff live team-total unders unless model median is at least 4.0 points below the live line, or 5.5 points below if the team already has 55+ at halftime.
Reject live totals/team totals where the edge relies on a half-point below one normal scoring event unless price is +140 or better and pace is clearly declining.
When OKC pressure produces 7+ opponent turnovers by halftime or 14+ through 3Q, increase both OKC scoring expectation and opponent desperation/garbage scoring tail by +2 to +4 combined points.

When Luka is inactive and LAL rotation relies on Reaves/Kennard/Rui shooting, do not assume collapse equals suppression; maintain a late bench/spacing scoring tail unless game total pace collapses below threshold.
After multiple same-slate LiveFlow losses, reduce stake to minimum unit and require two independent signals before next strike.
patches_to_activate.source: User loss-streak note after OKC@LAL Game 3

rolling_window_use: High-weight Game 4 input. OKC pressure, second-half scoring acceleration, and Lakers one-basket team-total hook risk must be treated as active. Team-total unders against Lakers require larger buffer; OKC overs and game overs may deserve greater attention when Thunder pressure turns live-ball turnovers into pace inflation.
